analysis.name: R. Amzi Jeffs

review_highlights.comment: Confusing lectures, the textbook is clearer when explaining difficult concepts. Difficult exams and homework, very little partial credit given. Class is curved but prof is vague about specifics so hard to know where you stand.

He is a nice guy who cares about his students. His lectures were dull, and they were out of sync with the recitations. Graded by homeworks, 3 midterms and 1 final exam. Level of work expected is high, but still manageable. Wouldn't take him again for peace of mind
Nice guy. Useful OH. Lectures and homework are basic. Exam difficulty fluctuates a lot. Expect zero partial credit. Notes not recorded or posted anywhere. Would recommend another teacher for 21-228.

lectures are confusing, pushes heavy usage on textbook and youtube lectures. features extremely tough tests and assignments with minimal grading leniency, making it hard to gauge standing. 
avoid at all costs

Writes lecture on board, but it's hard to follow. Relied on textbook and YouTube lectures (search for "cmu 21-228" on YT). Homeworks and tests are very hard and there is no partial credit. I had no idea if I was learning the right things or how well I was doing in the class. Expect pain and suffering

analysis.name: Alex Reinhart
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: One of my favorite professors ever. I'm not sure why other students gave him such a low rating. That was NOT my experience at all. Professor Reinhart is fantastic and incorporates humor into his lectures. I learned a lot from him and I would highly recommend his classes.

This professor does not care about the students at all. The notes are completely useless with 0 explanation. Most of the students never go to class cuz u learn nothing lol. A student asked on piazza why a function equals to X and his response was indeed it is equal to X. How is he still teaching? I am honestly confused.

Negative experience overall. His lectures/notes are full of expressions that are not clearly defined mathematically. Hand waving explanations often led to confusions. I don't see a passion for teaching and students' learning in general.
He absolutely does not care about his students or their success. Worst professor I had during my time at CMU.

analysis.name: Noha Abdelghany
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Genuinely poor self awareness for students. Took exceptionally long to respond to any student requests in general. Very poor communication in general from her and it doesn't seem like she realizes it. Would not recommend taking her for concepts. Did cancel lecture a few times as well.

Prof. A shares detailed notes and always explains examples step-by-step in lectures. Weekly quizzes with no midterms average the workload over the semester. She's also very approachable in office hours.

Abdelghany is one of my favourite professors of all time. She really cares about her students, really passionate while teaching, and very willing to help (if you are able to find her in her OH). Homeworks imo are really hard, but you learn a lot.

Good lecturer. Expect long and difficult homeworks. DO NOT try to do this class alone, especially if this is your first real proof based math class. Her OH is useful and her TAs will not let you drown. Her tests are challenging, but fair.
doesnt reply to piazza posts and quite hard to reach outside very small windows of time, lectures are messy too and expects students to learn material for a midterm not discussed in lecture?

analysis.name: Andrew Hicks
analysis.verdict: Highly rated with a manageable workload
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 29 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: This guy is hilarious. He loves making as many pop culture references as possible in his lectures. Overall an amazing professor, and likes keeping in touch with students even from past colleges. Take him if you can, and acknowledge he is a good professor.

Hicks the goat is the best! clearest, most enthusiastic, most approachable professor I ever had, and yet he does it well and also no nonsense and is to the point all the times! content is hard but very doable and he gives bonuses! take advantage of them and PARTICIPATE! he loves when you participate and will help you! totally 6/5!

Professor Hicks fostered an engaging learning environment and clearly communicated both expectations and the essential course content. While he doesn't curve tests, he includes bonus questions that often reference unique points from his lectures, which encourages attendance and attentiveness. Overall, I would gladly take another class with him!

Dr. Hicks was one of the best professors I've had the privilege of taking a class with at CMU, and one of the best teachers I've had throughout my life. He had not only the ability to convey class material to us in an approachable and digestable manner, but also was able to connect with students on a personal level through his sense of humor.

Hey guys!!! I really love Prof. Hicks so much! He explains things very clearly and is so passionate about teaching, gives nice pop culture bonuses and math bonuses, clearly a man who cares about your success! 10/5! I love you so much Dr. Hicks and you made my excel at calc III much more! Best math prof. I ever had! I will miss you soon! A++ prof!

analysis.name: Anne Kohlbrenner
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Anne is an awesome lecturer. She answers any questions you have and is very respectful (she once stayed back after lecture for nearly 40 minutes answering questions and explaining advanced topics). While the course is very difficult, she does a phenomenal job of making the concepts easier to understand.
Excellent lectures. Always patiently answering questions. The course is certainly difficult even you have a lot of prior knowledge, but the professor is always helpful.

Absolutely phenomenal lectures and will do anything to help her students in any way possible. Always answers questions in the best sense for her students to understand the concept of that week if it means to stay after lecture for any amount of time. She also gives guidance of how to do well mentally with the programming assignments.
She is an amazing lecturer. She is also really funny. She is enthusiastic, passionate, extremely knowledgeable, and very kind to her students.

analysis.name: David Anderson
analysis.verdict: Tough course, but students consistently rate the teaching highly, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 41 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: This guy made me like physics. I did not like physics and now I do. Taking him again this semester just because it's him. Please if you have the chance, take Dr. Anderson's classes.

He is one of the best professors at the university. He cares for both the content of the lectures and the well being of students. He makes time to help you with concepts if you need and finds ways to keep lectures engaging. He is also always eager to point you to other resources and follow up with you. A true example.
Absolutely one of the greatest professors at CMU.
A good guy. He'll really help you out if you go to office hours. I like how he focuses on the concepts instead of the numbers. Gets you ready to derive it yourself when you forget.

Anderson is like the inspiring British father you wish you had at CMU.  GO TO HIS OFFICE HOURS, lectures are good but no one is tryna get up at 9 am MWF so use his hours for extra help.  He gives quizzes on Fridays so be ready for that, but otherwise he is super fair and the way he approaches physics is very new and creative; not focused on numbers

analysis.name: Aryn Gittis
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment:
This professor forced you to attend in-class discussion. If she saw you not speaking, she would call you out. Also result of discussion will be written down in notes for grading.
Her slides are useless. You have to look at her write-up on the board and handwritting is messy and chaotic. 
Her exam is difficult. Heavy point deduction is expected.

This professor consistently expects you to be able to incorporate nuanced information into your answers without her specifically asking for it. Very unclear 90% of the time, which is a shame because the material is interesting. Often gets frustrated/annoyed when students ask clarifying questions. Writes info on board, so ppts are very unhelpful.
She is great at explaining things and I can tell her enthusiasm for the materials in her lectures.

Work itself isn't bad. email communication w/her is frustrating+unhelpful. She failed to clarify/write the expectations for the neurotransmitter project+took a month (since we already submitted work for project) for her to claim these expectations. Many ppl have insufficient time to redo work to meet the new expectations nearing finals season.

Her course was probably the most difficult one for this semester. She doesn't take attendance but there are group exercises after each lecture that are graded. Also her slides do not have any info, you need to go to actually get any notes. Tests were all take home but were so difficult. Wanted to go to oh before a test and she wasn't there

analysis.name: Andrew Johnson
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I spent a summer apprenticing with Andrew, and he was awesome. Really knowledgeable, witty, and pushed me to think out the box in bold ways.

Class: Concepts 1(disregard if you got him for drawing) If you want an easy class, dont take Johnson, if you want to push the boundaries of what you thought you could do,take Johnson's. He's crazy, disjointed, we don't follow a lesson plan we learn alot and think even more. He will help if you ask. I recommend him for anyone thinking art

Often loses temper on students and gets too close, acts as if he knows what is best for them without actually thinking about other people's taste.  I wanted to learn photoshop and it was the main topic, but the course lost focus and everyone just did random projects.

He is easily the most inspiring, well read, and enthusiastic art teachers I have ever had. Out of the box with a super duper loose curriculum. Any day he can just decide "its too stale in here, we're gonna go outside and draw weird things. One thing, dont ask him for clarification on his thought process, artistically he moves a mile a minute.
nasty, negative -- helpful if you let him be, though. he's tough, so you gotta be tough too.

analysis.name: Anil Ada
analysis.verdict: Tough course, but students consistently rate the teaching highly, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 22 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Ada is the best professor ever. Amazing teacher, really knows his stuff and really knows how to teach. Any negative things you may have heard about the course have nothing to do with him (he only makes it better). It's hard - but super fun and rewarding. If you read the notes and prepare well you'll get an A.

Hes a hard-working professor! He's easily one of the best professors I've had. He transformed a really hard class where everyone gets a C and they just curve it into a hard class that you learn in and you work hard for that B. My personal opinion but I think hes really charming (heart eyes)

Best professor in my life ever, period. I would honestly say that Ada was born to teach 251. Absolutely amazing lecture, he knows what he's doing, genuinely cares about teaching and students learning. I would take 251 over and over again just because of him. I would go as far to say that his lectures are life-changing.
Amazing professor. Truly wants kids to learn, and will meet you more than halfway. If you put the effort in, you will succeed in this class.
Great teacher and an even cooler guy. Taught the class very well and made the effort to get to know students that attended office hours (at least from the times I went).

analysis.name: Kim Beck

review_highlights.comment: My experience in Beck's printmaking class was extremely disappointing. When questions were asked, responses often felt dismissive rather than helpful, which created an unwelcoming environment for learning. I left the course feeling frustrated and under-supported.
Very self involved and definitively pretentious.
I do not follow all the negative reviews, and I found her to be quite sweet and caring. Above that, I did not feel like she wasted my time.

What a disappointment to be around faculty like this. Impossible to follow, rambling and incoherent style. Not sure if this was a class or just someone taking up time. I spent 4k to take a class with Kim and learned nothing.

A decent teacher, but very disorganized and classes were mostly improvised. She often didn't give out important class-related information on time, and the grades she put in for my projects differed greatly from what she told me about them in class. Most of my work was digital, so she didn't fully understand the medium either.

analysis.name: Brooke McCartney
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Frequently answers student questions with "I don't know the answer to that."  Teaches as if her class is the only course that students have to manage in the semester.  She will introduce a weeks worth of material the class before the exam.  Mandatory lectures where she just regurgitates a textbook that someone else wrote.

Assigns two (2) homeworks to be due the class before an exam.  5 minutes left in class before an exam, and she is excited to show us an entirely new mechanism that we are expected to know for the exam.  Often gets defensive when students ask questions.

Cell bio was the hardest CMU bio class I have experienced. We moved through material quickly and needed to memorize the lectures verbatim. Grading criteria was often unclear. One semester later, I can also say this class was the single most helpful class that I have taken at CMU. Ask questions, Dr. McCartney is kind and thorough in her explanations

Worst prof I've had at CMU, condescending (if you're struggling "just study more"), uncompromising (no makeup exams period), and overloads slides with textbook content verbatim, only to explain it even worse. Exams aren't a test of knowledge but rather a guessing game trying to figure out what she's put on the rubric (last exam best grade was a 76)

Teaches as if she's trying to prove how smart she is.  Her (mandatory) lectures would be helpful if you didn't know how to read; they are just copied directly from a textbook which she tries to explain, but worse.  The textbook is the extent of her knowledge- if you ask for more depth or clarification, she does not know.

analysis.name: Zach Branson
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Amazing professor, lectures are wonderful, lecture notes are super good. Actually tried to make the course better for students by looking at their feedbacks. Respects and cares about students.
One of the best lecturers here at CMU. Makes a lot of efforts to improve teaching quality. Definitely recommended.

He is super understanding and wants to help and support you even if you're doing really badly. I took a class with him a few years ago during a really hard time in my life and he did his best to work with me. He tries to set up the class with as much support as possible, both from him and also from classmates.

Professor Branson is an angel from heaven itself. He was an amazing lecturer and took the time to learn his student's names without even talking to them first. He also really listened to student feedback and incorporated it into his teaching. A real gem.

analysis.name: Silvia Borzutzky
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining

review_highlights.comment: This is the worst policy class I've taken. She is strongly biased and dismissive of views that differ from her own. She fails to create an open environment, and students feel afraid to speak up if their opinions don't match hers.

Her lectures are boring, but her assignments are easy. She likes you to spit back her opinions verbatim. She is EXTREMELY stubborn and views herself as superior to students. A good grade is not worth the stress of her personality.

Silvia is a phenomenal instructor - best I've had in this subject area. I considered myself a good writer prior to this class, but her assignments have led to visible improvement. I disagree with some of the other posts - she welcomes opposing views but has little tolerance for poor analysis. You need to properly support your arguments.

Silvia is an incredible professor. She practically knows everything, and she's incredibly sweet. There's a lot of reading but you never have to do it, since she comes to class with handouts on everything you need to know. But her course material is super interesting, as are the class discussions. Add anything to those and she'll be pleased.
a little boring, but its not too hard if you do the reading..the class discussions can be very interesting

analysis.name: Roy Briere
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining

review_highlights.comment: Incredibly smart and knows a lot about the subject but is extremely condescending and sarcastic. Makes you feel dumb whenever you ask a question
Very Funny. Really likes Diet Dr. Pepper. Exams are (imo) fair but HEFTY. Homework is kinda tedious.

Is clearly very smart but doesn't always try to explain things in ways everyone can understand. Makes occasional obnoxious comments. Goes very fast. Is very confusing. Office hours are important but if you don't think exactly the way he does, makes you feel dumb. Basically, the class is curved and most people struggle a lot.

Had him for physics of musical sound.  Not a difficult class, but lectures were very clear.  Occasionally dry, but always made sense.  Generally personable and humorous as well, but I can see why some people might be put off by his style.

great guy, fast-paced class if you're not ready for it. his lecture notes(doesn't tolerate skipping class w/o reason)and **practice exams** are super helpful. study for the midterms, he broadcasts whats on those. final exam is essentially made for geniuses, i dropped a letter grade after the final because it's impossible. he likes boba & dr pepper.

analysis.name: Bonnie Nozari

She intentionally structures classes so that you learn as much as possible and it stays with you, but without making it super stressful in terms of grading and tests
Way too much effort for a gen ed

analysis.name: Burak Ozdoganlar

review_highlights.comment: Great lecturer. Fun professor.
He is such a good teacher! Super cool and funny
A really, really excellent teacher - one of my best at CMU. If you work with him and take all the opportunities for extra work that he gives, you'll certainly do wonderfully.

Burak is a great professor. Lectures are clear and he gives plenty of practice for exam. He also teaches you to use Matlab for more than plotting, and it's the first core course that does so. However, he could do better at providing outside help, as office hours are very limited.
Dr. Oz is a great teacher and his lectures are very useful.

analysis.name: Michael Bockstaller
analysis.verdict: Highly rated with a manageable workload

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Bockstaller grades very generously, as long as you can finish his homework very closely. He is also very kind in quizzes and exams grading. Even though I was not able to understand the course content completely, probably because he is better at teaching higher level courses, I could easily get a high score. Definitely will take again.
This dude looks like Arnold Schwarzenegger, and totally sounds like him too. It just makes the classes so much more fun
His lectures are very engaging, and he makes the class very enjoyable. Reading the textbook will help a lot in fully understanding the material.
This professor is extremely clear and engaging. His lectures are consistent, but be ready to read the textbook for clarification and studying.
Great professor! Very fun and enthusiastic. Plus, his awesome Austrian accent is entertaining.

analysis.name: Bill Nace
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 26 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Don't be fooled by the clarity of the lectures, this professor is the definition of inconsistency. The homework assignments are REALLY long, the questions that he asks in the homework and during the examinations are ambiguous. He cares more about giving you a hard time, than helping  you understand the meaning of the material. I hated his class.

The tough grading is not really a problem if you pay attention in lecture and go to office hours. Sure, the exams are hard but you are provided with a mock exam and you can bring a notes sheet. This class definitely takes a lot of work and requires a significant time commitment, but it is a core class and should be treated with that in mind.

Absolutely ridiculous, he is unreasonable in assignments, exams, and projects. He  is not very clear on his requirements and grades harshly despite being unclear. This course relied heavily on trivia.  A complete waste of time. There are better courses to take in the same topic especially in the CS department. This is true of most ECE classes.
He is RUDE and not clear what he wants!!!

RUNNNNN from this class. He made me hate hardware engineering. He loves what he is teaching, but the lectures are mediocre at best. Lectures don't help you do well on the hw or exams. The lectures are pretty useless TA's help you learn so much. All he cares about is giving u a hard time on the exams. You can view the whole class as a trial by fire.

analysis.name: Tom Cortina

review_highlights.comment: Absolutely horrific and unhelpful. Tanked my QPA
Lectures are well-structured and well-paced. Really knows his stuff. Very clear during lecture. Funny guy, has great presence.

He's really a great guy. He handles the class like children far too often for him to hold my interest, but his class is very easy and you do learn some interesting things. He gives a quiz every week, but they are a joke so as long as you go you'll get an A. He makes it very easy to get good grades on everything. And he is always open to talk.

Cortina is the man. Easily one of the best professors at CMU. Actually teaches concepts and applications, and does so very well. The class he teaches is very easy if you have already coded in your life, but if you are new to computer science, then Cortina is the guy for you. He will make you learn, and you will enjoy it.
Gives very clear explanation, very funny guy - and after his class I understood that CS in my way to follow

Failed to support students in his department and created a hostile culture at the school of art- shouldn't have been allowed to teach.
Couldn't run the department or his own career, just filled in the gaps left by others and pretended to care about art when in reality he clearly just cared about nothing.

analysis.name: Clayton Merrell

review_highlights.comment: Clayton is amazing. He has taught about every kind of art student there can possibly be, so he immediately knows what you're trying to do and how to help guide you to your goal. He also knows a lot about pretty much every artist ever to feature in the annals of art history, so he always has good recommendations for each student. He's also very kind

I mean I now know more useless color theory facts than any human being should reasonably carry around. Like, thank you so much for teaching me how “warm tones advance and cool tones recede,” Clayton. Super helpful. If I ever need to overthink why blue next to orange is “vibrating,” I'll let you know.

Clayton's class was extremely disappointing. His approach to teaching painting felt disengaged and unstructured, and I rarely received meaningful feedback or guidance that could actually help me grow. For a program that promises artistic development and faculty support, this experience fell far short of expectations.

The professor struggles to communicate concepts clearly, often jumping between topics without structure. Lectures feel disorganized, explanations are vague, and questions are sometimes dismissed, making it difficult to understand or engage with the material.
Clearly not qualified for this position, pretty much the type of teacher you'd expect to find at a community college.

analysis.name: Clive Newstead
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 17 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Be prepared to learn beyond just memorization and repeated steps to solve certain types of problems - this course pushes you to truly abstract the concepts you learn. That being said, Clive was an incredible professor whose lectures were funny and thorough. Resources are available to you and Clive is a super smart guy who is always willing to help.

Concepts can be a fast moving course and the problem sets really challenge you to your limits. Clive is a very entertaining and intelligence professor who's always open to help, so make sure you take advantage of the course resources and budget your time wisely.

Pretty good lecturer, but his (four) midterms were impossible to finish in 50 minutes and barely curved. Probably would have been more motivated to learn if I wasn't destroyed by a midterm every 3 weeks. 
Wouldn't have rated this guy this low but his test-taking approach and stubbornness made me hate vector calculus.

Not easy but not awful. I learned a ton. PSETs are hard but exams are OK if your prepared for them. Lectures are clear, he pauses to answer everyones questions and explains things in lots of different ways. I wish there was more CS content but we learned about gradient descent.

He is a nice person but creates super difficult tests that are time crunches. He also grades harshly then refuses to release statistics about the grades to the class. If you have a choice between 259 and 266, choose 259.

analysis.name: Jae Choi
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, and ratings have been declining

review_highlights.comment: At first, he seems like a very nice dude. That is, until you take one of his midterms. He will lie straight to your face about content that is going on the midterms, and some of the material on the midterm will be covered in the lecture after the midterm itself. It is safe to assume that if you ask a question, around 7/10 times he is lying to you.

he lied to the entire class on multiple occasions especially regarding our midterm. He also provides ZERO feedback as to how well you're doing in the course, resulting in multiple people dropping the course even though they would've ended with As or Bs. Never take a course with him, for your own good.

Super contradictory about guidelines in the course. When asking questions about the course, most of the time he lies. He frequently puts stuff on midterms that haven't been covered yet, and he often lies straight to your face when asked about content that is going to be on midterms.

Really great guy when you get to know him. Go to his OH, ask questions in class, he truly cares and wants to help. I heard he was a tough grader/gave hard exams but I think it's better now. One of the best math profs I have had here. He's a hidden gem.

Awful teacher. 
Does not teach material properly but the hw is extremely long and difficult. Avoid him
He rambled for the entire class period. I would have taken the class and just read the textbook to learn the material, but he required attendance and didn't use a textbook. Painful.

I learned a lot in this class. Statistical Computing (36-350) is most useful if you've done no or little programming, but I'd recommend it to anyone who needs to learn R.

All things considered, Collins does a pretty good job covering the extremely wide range of material in lectures (albeit a bit confusing sometimes). Turning problem sets in person is only slightly inconvenient. Tests/homework always have reasonable questions and grading. Good guy.

Not a single  grade is dropped and the tests are fairly difficult. The only time I actually learned anything was in recitation and the lectures are mostly theory

nice guy but assigns diabolical quantities of homework that barely pertain to the weekly exams. has unreasonably high expectations of students and exams have too difficult of content for people to perform even at a mediocre level. lied to the class about avg grades from past years being better to make them feel bad and study harder. exam avg of 50%

Avoid, take Anderson or wait for an alternative. Collins requires more than necessary. He gives very little leniency and requires more work than what is necessary. He is a decent lecturer, but fails at being a good professor. He can be not engaging at times and is not understanding of students other commitments.

Glad I took the course while she only taught the labs, though seemed to me her TAs were doing most of the work. review_highlights.comment: There's not technically that much work. Do yourself a favor, read the notes, do the practice, and don't get behind. It's not that hard to do well if you stay on top of it.

Teaches some material in class, but most is meant to be learned on your own. She cares a lot about students understanding, but due to the difficult nature of the content, it is still a hard class. There is a lot of practice in class with mechanisms which is good.

absolutely loved the way she used lectures to go over problems instead of introducing new content. she made a notoriously difficult class very doable. there were never really any curveballs on the exams. highly recommend!

So for Silva with OChem I, the first 5 -7 weeks will be pretty easy and you'll get in the habit of not worrying about the class very much. You can do the homework the night before. Then she crams mechanisms and reactions into the last half which works but if you don't step up your game, you won't be prepared.   She has great lecture notes online.

"Flipped-class" model so you're expected to teach yourself the material and participate in solving example problems in class. She seems to know everyone's name, even if you barely speak to her, which I think shows her dedication to the class. Extremely generous grading and ample extra credit opportunities.

analysis.name: Kim Hyatt

review_highlights.comment:

这个老师的课需要你花时间，但有付出最终应该都不会差

Missing it, due to tech issues or otherwise, meant losing 30 points. She shamed struggling students and said, “we all have the same 24 hours.” Very disappointed.
Gave vague feedback/instructions, very petty when you ask questions, felt like her class was less about developing presentation skills and more about catering to her.
This is the worst I've ever seen, yes, the very worst. Not deserve to be a teacher at all. And that person who gave a 5-star rating, are you serious? Were you asked to boost the ratings?

Taught me ways to be a better learnwr
don't ever let her enter your life!!!

Before I took this class, I didn't have much interest in thermodynamics. This professor is just awesome - he explains things amazingly well, and you can tell he has a genuine passion for the subject. He is very willing to help any student that has questions. Very hard material, but by exam time I felt like I had learned a ton. Keep it up Hendrich!

Prof. Hendrich was my favorite teacher, but he's is really hard.  Before I took his class I barely knew what an electrob was and now I've switched my major to Theology.  He's dreamy, he reminds me of an athletic Conan O'Brian, sans the attitude.  Overall, awesome teacher, Old Soul.  Rock on MPH.

He is very inspirational - an extremely passionate teacher.  He can rotate a matrix like no one else, when he talked about tensors he almost saw my O-face.  Before I took this class, I thought ENDOR was where the Ewoks lived.  He has completely perturbed my world.

Hardcore-ness. Kind of ridiculous material and exams. He explains things reaaaallllyy well, but hes more of a concept guy, and the exams are all problems, so...make sure to go to rec and do LOTS of problems.

analysis.name: Gregory Johnson
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, and ratings have been declining

review_highlights.comment: Greggo is one of the few human beings I've seen who can write as fast as he speaks. Multivariable calculus with him made for painful note taking, so don't even try to copy what he writes in time, listen to him instead. He's very theory based, and often leaves you teaching yourself how to actually solve problems, but the concepts will be understood.

Took calc-3d with Greggo in Fall 2014. Very clear lectures. Listens to student feedback. After the first midterm some students said the homework was a little too much, and he reduced the amount by around  40%. Gives extra-credit problems on every midterm. Exams are not hard and reflects the materials taught. Definitely take his 259. Awesome prof.

Lectures are clear, and recitation worksheets are a great supplement to lectures. Homework assignments are most of the time long, but not very hard. Listens to feedback. Three midterms and a final. Gives extra credit on every midterm. The first two were easy, but the third was unexpectedly hard(class avg was 45, and 60+ was an A). Final was easy.

One of the best professors I've had here. He is extremely clear in everything. If you go to him, he gives great feedback, but you usually won't need to because there's opportunity for that in recitation and HW is returned with detailed comments of what to improve on. Also, he is genuinely good at communicating concepts, unlike some professors.

Great professor! I took Calc1 and Calc2 in highschool, so I thought it would be easy to transition to Calc3 at CMU. I was wrong and Greggo really made me work hard. I bombed my first test but got a lot better at calculus as the course progressed. He is a very clear professor, and he challenges you on the tests. I ended up loving the class though.

analysis.name: Jeff Hinkelman
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently

review_highlights.comment: Jeff is just a real cool dude. I have taken four of his classes. Each one has pushed me and the class to think in bold and interesting ways, and has covered many important and thought-provoking topics. Awesome guy.

Jeff is AWESOME I would absolutely recommend taking a class with him. He's very engaging, knew everyone's names after the first week and called on most people every class. Super interesting and knowledgable guy!

Jeff honestly cares about his students. The classes I've taken with him have been my favorite, despite it being nowhere affiliated with my major. His seminars are smaller so participation is essential to get the most out of the class but he makes you feel so comfortable that participation isn't a problem.

What an amazing professor! Jeff was kind and funny and as someone who didn't take Intro when entering the class, he was able to create an environment where I wasn't shy to share my opinions on the films we watched. I highly recommend taking his classes!!
Probably the best professor I've had at CMU. Humorous, engaging, and approachable with just the right amount of intimidation. Would highly recommend taking a class with him.

He's guaranteed to cram your head with knowledge, and you can tell he puts in a lot of work when making the homework assignments. As you go through then, the techniques used and results found ever so gingerly pushes you to the next question's answer. It's brilliant. He's brilliant.

21-370 is a discrete class. While I get the materials in the first 3/4 of the semester, I'm destroyed in the last 2-3 weeks when he RUSHED through materials in CONTINUOUS time (beyond 370). He seems to just wanna be done with the class than to let students LEARN about anything. Don't respond well to feedback. Require to code which is not a pre-req.

He is not always prepared for lectures and has very messy handwriting. Sometimes he tends to go all over the place without logistics and order. He's not good at asking for feedback and response to them. Maybe he has a higher expectation for us with good intentions. But I hope he can set a higher standard for himself in teaching FIRST.

analysis.name: Rubab Jafry O'Connor
analysis.verdict: Highly rated with a manageable workload, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 17 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Professor O'Connor truly cares about students' personal and professional development, rather than simply academic. You will be pushed outside your comfort zone in this class, but it is truly a course where you get out what you put in.

BLE with Professor O'Connor is a course that truly prepares you for post-grad. It is a class where you will get out what you put in, but there are many opportunities for networking with alumni, resume building, interview practice, and public speaking. This class builds your soft skills in a way that is directly translatable to the real world.

Professor O'Connor is very personable with her students and makes a distinct effort to learn and encourage everyone on their professional journey. Her material is focused on classroom discussion, with a focus on improving soft skills. Do not be surprised if you have a spontaneous classroom assignment where you must speak for five minutes.

The BLE classes are short, but they will push you outside of your comfort zone. There is one assignment a week, and it can typically be completed in less than an hour. Working ahead will make your life much easier.

As a professor, Rubab was always there to help you and was one of the first people I went to when I needed job advice.

Professor O'Connor is an amazing teacher and inspirational leader in our school community. I have learned so much from her so far, and I am sure I will continue to. Her class is engaging and encourages active participation from everyone. Her assignments allow you to reflect and challenge yourself. Overall, a fantastic course.

He is an amazing professor. He really wants you to do well, and at the same time is very funny. He however changes the syllabus frequently and all this means is that you have to do a lot of last minute work. But, if you do the work, you will be rewarded with A's easily.

absolutely amazing professor and lecturer. he's so funny but also a great teacher. super kind and understanding too. if you've taken any sort of psych class before the class will be a breeze, if not you may have to read the textbook
bro just makes the course hard for no reason and cannot lecture

no. simply. no. lectures are just him reading from slides. he says everything is trivial, but it is not. homework is much harder than questions gone over in lectures or recitations, so I now live in OH. homework takes at least 15 hours on a really good week.

Definitely recommend if you have the mathematical maturity

The course is inspirational, but lectures are pretty fast-paced, and biweekly quizzes can sometimes be overwhelming if you have other deadlines/midterms coming along. I ground through the course and somehow regretted it.

He truly knows what he's talking about. His homeworks and exams are quite tough, but he teaches you to the point where the material gets very engaging. Really defines the true meaning of an honors level math class.

A is achievable and the professor is very kind.

Pay attention to lectures and homework, make sure to attend class as well as there's often supplemental tips that can really help with understanding the material, especially since there's no textbook.
Attend classes and take advantage of the practice material within recitations!

Professor Lessem was a great teacher! Her exams are easy as long as you take notes, do the homework, and do the practice exams. She's also very willing to help during office hours, if you ever have questions on any of the material.

analysis.name: David Kosbie
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, and ratings have been declining

review_highlights.comment: Says this course is his child and yet class time is used to do everything but cover the material we need to know... K

Instead of giving me helpful feedback, he just shamed me for having social anxiety, and I can't see him the same way again. I hope I never have to take one of his classes again.

I think his class time could definitely be used more productively. The basis of a flipped classroom is that it offers class time to reinforce material, and that has not happened as much as I would have hoped so far. Otherwise, great class, offers you every possible chance to succeed and puts his heart into it.

David Kosbie is hands down the greatest teacher I have had in my entire education. 15-115 -- Fundamentals of Programming and Computer Science -- has been the most rewarding, enriching, inspirational, character building, and difficult course i've taken in my life. 15-112 is the most challenging course I've ever taken in my academic career, but it is also my favorite (despite being a Business major!). Professor Kosbie challenges you to excel not only as a programmer, but as a human being. His passion for living is infectious. By the end of this course, you will emerge with a new view on life beyond academia.

THE BEST PROFESSOR!!!!

Washburn is a nice guy, and he is trying very hard. But there are definitely ways he can be more effective at teaching. First off, he doesn't follow the textbook, which is frustrating. I had difficulties while in her class, and she was so understanding and helpful in meeting my needs. She was accessible outside of class for feedback and to go over material. Professor Simons also gave me by far the best writing feedback Ive ever received from an educator. Knows her field in incredible depth and cares enough to share it with you

This course has changed the way I SEE the world, in every aspect of my life. The things I learned about design and typography, about visual and verbal communication, will affect my career and my personal life forever. Karen comes across as very kind and nurturing, but she is a fierce and brilliant scholar who expects the same from her students.

I have taken 3 classes taught by Karen Kornblum Berntsen, simply to get as much wisdom and insight from her as possible. She is the best teacher I've ever had in any subject. Karen has the ability to pull the curtain back and bring her students directly in contact with their most intelligent and creative selves.

Not very good at giving feedback, and often focuses on minor details vs. the big picture.  You'll spend a lot of time of a project and be super proud of it, and have the entire thing discounted because a shade of purple is slightly off, or she doesn't like the text of a sub-headline.  Prone to hyperbole.

Very nice and caring person. Passionate about her work.  However, super head in the clouds with a bleeding heart.  Need somewhere to go for thanksgiving? She's there. Need a concise, consistent explanation of what she expects from the assignment ? Not so much.

Good professor and a great person with concerns about students who work sincerely. The course is very heavy- challenging HWs, project and quizzes that eat up the whole weekend. However if you put in adequate work, the outcomes are worth it.

I have never seen a professor care so much about ensuring the students learn. He explains concepts very well and is very patient. Understands the difficulties faced by students and is very approachable.

As a remainder, for summer 2024, final: 30%, lab: 50%, wa: 20%, in case he decides to make things more difficult for others.

Undergrads: take this if you want pain.

Curtis wrote the textbook for the course and so it contains better versions of the lecture notes (more in-depth derivations, often). Homework is very helpful for learning the content.

Great Guy. Lectures are kinda dry but overall good. You don't really have to go to class since the textbook just has the exact same notes but more in depth, but he makes attendance a grade. The tests and homework are pretty chill once you get the hang of the types of problems. Final project was chill. Overall great
It's great that he wrote the textbook for M&I, but about 20% of worked problems and examples are wrong.

review_highlights.comment: TBH: Cuonzo is not your average math teacher; he's an extraordinary maestro in the world of matrix algebra. With an infectious passion for the subject and a knack for making complex concepts understandable, he stands as a beacon of inspiration and knowledge in the classroom. Rate: 11/10
Cuonzo really knows his stuff and is very caring; however, sometimes I get lost in his eyes because he is so attractive.

review_highlights.comment: Class was a joke.  Dude showed random video clips from movies that were only very weakly connected to the lecture in order to win over the younger crowd.  I learned nothing other than that I don't want anything to do with this guy.

Professor more interested in making friends than making a meaningful lesson. Culbertson's course is essentially just him reciting stories and giving students gifts in an attempt win favor. All in all, the class gave me little help when I started my own company the following year. Go find another entrepreneurship class, you will be glad you did.
Intro to Entrep. was the best course I've taken at CMU thanks to R.F.  Incredible professor, and a great guy who really cares about his students.
To be honest I found him biased towards some people. He talks about unrelated things. Will not take any of his classes again.
Great guy, hilarious, easy-going. Talk up and he'll love you.

analysis.name: James Cummings
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: I am a recent graduate who had Discrete Mathematics with Cummings.  The grading was not clear, and at times his explanations were very unclear.  He was not accessible at all and never returned a single email.

Prof. Cumming's lectures are clear and easy to follow, although I hope he could go a little faster. He is very articulate and explains subtle things well. He is very willing to help, offers hints/comments on proofs if you email him. Midterm and Final are take-home, and the problems are pretty standard. Definitely recommend.

Professor Cummings is a wonderful teacher and is one  of the most approachable teachers in the math department.  He likes to do a lot of his lessons on the fly, but this is not a problem because he can prove almost anything off the top of his head.  He loves to help and is very easy going.

Probably the first genius level professor I've taken a class under. But be absolutely sure to go to his lectures consistently or you will fall behind fast, and it is very hard to get up to pace. Nevertheless, he is very approachable and kindhearted.

His teaching style is very difficult to follow and often times his lectures had nothing to do with the problem sets assigned. Teaches in a very abstract way and hardly provides examples during his lecture.

analysis.name: David Shumway
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Very poor lectures—he will ask vague questions with an expected answer and become frustrated and rude when students don't immediately guess the point he wants to make. Essays had very few comments on them that offered little insight, and he is very unresponsive outside of class. Material he offers during lectures is easily found in any textbook.

I genuinely can't comprehend why he's put in charge of so many mandatory classes. 
I swear, this guy gets a power trip out of pulling the most obscure detail out of readings for final exams. Participation is mandatory but don't expect to engage in any meaningful dialect either. Feels like taking a high school class if the teacher had a massive ego.

Often wrong about the material. Students had to point to context and teach him in class because it seemed as if he had not deeply understood many of his own readings. I would recommend taking someone more knowledgable if possible.

His approach was heavy on pretension but light on substance, giving the impression of rigor without delivering real educational value. The structure of the course often felt like a hollow approximation of what a college class should be, but without meaningful content to back it up.

The film analysis is more "learn by doing", or more accurately "learn by watching him do" for large portions of class. This may work for many but if you are looking for a systemic approach you will be disappointed. He will ask very broad questions and expect a specific answer (and sometimes be annoyed by the class not guessing the answer).

analysis.name: Daniela Mihai
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit

analysis.name: David Handron
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 20 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: This is a standard class. He has good slides and posts them on canvas. That said, I had a problem with how he treated me as a student. I needed more time to finish a HW due to my grandpa's funeral. I asked him for more time and to drop the late penalty, but he refused. I was very disappointed, especially since he presents himself as a family man.

I found Handron frustrating for several reasons. First off, he removes much of the more mature mathematical notation from his notes and homework, making theorems and problems overly complicated and wordy. In addition, his tests focus not on deep understanding, but on your ability to do elementary calculus and simple algebra extremely quickly.

I had to miss an exam due to a family emergency, which I told him about as soon as I knew. When I came later that week to take my make-up exam, he told me that he had "forgotten I scheduled the make-up exam until 20 minutes ago" and that he "hadn't even printed the test out yet." I was offered no extensions on that week's assignments either.

He tries his best, and is very kind, but manages to overcomplicate topics to such a degree that his lectures often take away from my learning rather than help. His lectures are more focused on mindless algebraic manipulations rather than intuition or practice problems.

Uses slides from previous years, and goes relatively fast through them. It's more important to follow them rather than writing everything down. Very clear and a lot of good outside help is available. HWs can be challenging but are very reasonable. Plenty of review problems for tests and no surprises on them. Highly recommend.

Professor Shaw is an absolutely astounding professor, easily my favorite one from my six years at Carnegie Mellon.  I took every architecture class of hers that I could, and loved them all.  She makes it about more than just what's in the textbook: you learn about culture, the other arts, and history as well.  So much admiration for her!

Diane Shaw inspired me continuously throughout my architectural education @ Carnegie Mellon. By far my favorite processor throughout my college experience. She has a way of making buildings come to life in a rich, passionate, funny way & challenged us to view architecture as a story. I took every class of hers that I could :)

Really funny, and really interested in what she teaches--and by virtue of that, makes it interesting to learn. Was only mildly curious about architectural history before I took the class, but the class turned out really interesting and even kind of fun. Don't miss a lecture if it can be helped.

Fully lecture-based. There is no interaction with students, which may make the lecture very boring and difficult to concentrate on. There were only three exams and no other assignments, which I personally think is not encouraging the learning process at all.

review_highlights.comment: Although some of the material was hard I always enjoyed going to lecture. He is a very caring professor, one time I submitted a big project late and he came up to me after class and asked if I needed any help and if everything was okay. Not just that but he is very charismatic. I really enjoyed having taken this core IS class with him.

Professor Lal has been by far my best professor at CMU. He is very charismatic and energetic when teaching the class. He provides very thorough feedback and truly cares about his students. He is very responsive to his email and always makes time for his students when they need help. Would definitely recommend classes he teaches.

Professor is so nice and understanding. He takes time to know his students and is willing to meet with anyone for any reason at any time, even late at night and during the weekends if needed. He gives good feedback and is very clear about project criteria.
He's really nice and understanding. He's not the most organized but he really takes time to help each student if they need help.

Difficult to understand him and syllabus structure is ambiguous. Feedback is subjective (to his experience). Dismisses an entire section of a submitted assignment if he deems the beginning unworthy of being read. Suggestions are often disregarded. Have to squeeze clear info about exams/homework out of him.

analysis.name: Deborah Brandon
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 21 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: One of my favorite professors. Goes to great lengths to make sure our enormous lecture does not have any questions about topics before moving on. She is always willing to answer questions a million ways until you understand. Brandon keeps Calculus light and occasionally fun--a great accomplishment. Go to lecture/recitation, ask questions, get an A.

Very good intro to calculus course. Should be super simple if you've had any calc background. Even if you haven't, prof Brandon is wonderful and begs the students to ask questions. She makes herself available if you need any help and is very thoughtful. If you get an A on all three exams you don't have to take the final, which is super nice.

Excellent professor. The course can be very hard, but the way she teaches it makes it much easier. Her notes are VERY helpful. This is definitely one of those few courses where you don't want to skip the lectures. Review sessions for exams are HELPFUL!

Best calc teacher I ever had.  Encyclopedic coverage of topics, but always willing to walk you through stuff.  Expects you to learn, and works to support that.  It's an easy class if you work hard, and a hard class if you treat it like it's easy.

One of the best calc professors I've had. Excellent at lecturing, and would always be able to clear up any confusion you had on concepts. Had very realistic expectations of students, and was fun and energetic in class. Exams were fairly easy.

review_highlights.comment: I really enjoyed this class. Prof Kaynar made this class worthwhile. She loves the material and makes every effort to help the students find a passion for it as well. She is the sweetest professor and always wants to answer questions.

Professor Dilsun is a nice lady who seems to actually care about her students. I rarely went to lecture because of how amazing the course notes were, but every time I went she was able to explain things very clear and took time to answer the many questions I had during and after lecture.

Great professor and lectures are very clear. The class itself is interesting, although the homeworks, particularly the writtens, are very mundane. But that's not so much a problem with Dilsun but rather with the class as a whole. Would recommend choosing this instructor if possible for the class.

Kaynar is such a wonderful professor. She truly cares about her students which is a rarity. Despite the class being incredibly difficult, Kaynar goes the extra mile to ensure all her students have the resources necessary to being successful

Dilsun is very kind and always answers student questions without making them feel dumb. The assignments and midterms are the same as Iliano's so the class is still really tough, but Dilsun makes lectures a little better

Attendance is REALLY important on negotiation days.
An absolutely wonderful professor... highly would recommend taking any course he teaches in the future!
Great Prof. very passionate and it shows. His assignments and lectures are fun and enthralling.

analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Fairly difficult lecture-based class, but she gives a TON of extra credit to offset that. If you do all the extra credit, you get 3 points added to your final grade (so an 87 becomes a 90). There are 4 exams and you can drop one grade entirely, if you have a good enough grade you can skip the final. Got an A without getting above an 85 on an exam.

Really good lecturer and inspiring to have as a professor. I really enjoyed having Prof. Downs as a professor. However, the exams are pretty difficult, and if you do poorly or average, it can sentence your grade to being an overall B.

lectures are really fun and interesting. It's a pleasure to listen to her lectures. HOWEVER, tests are incredibly hard. All tests are fill-in blanks or short answer, which makes it harder than ever. You'll have to figure out what to put in the blanks. Very confusing, super super hard.

The lectures are fun and informative, HOWEVER, the exams are awful. There are many midterms, and there are many questions based on readings that she never mentioned in class or in homework so students have no chance to practice those completely unexpected questions. Most questions from the exams are fill-in-blank questions that are super confusing.

Lectures are really interesting but her tests are very unclear. They can be hard to study for and her test format takes awhile to get used to but the class is definitely manageable. A great class to take if you're wondering what decision science is about; you learn a lot of generally applicable information.

His power point slides are extremely unorganized also.

open book/open notes exams, 2 open laptop exams (geez), but he's not an exciting or great professor, uses same transparency, bad writing,..easy grader, I guess, but learn stuff..ehh...he gives no practice exams and he wrote our iffy text book.....

Had Domach for Spring Intro to Chem Engineering. Homework is challenging but minimal, tests are a bit harder. Goes through book examples on the board, to clarify if you don't understand the method. Likes to put in real-world applications, such as biotechnology and using distillation to make moonshine.

You really have to fight and argue your way through this class.

He is a very self-aware professor, eager to improve his teaching style to suit the students. He wants students to get something out of his class and if they put in the required effort they will. His classes will require thought and effort but he is willing to help and change his teaching to suit the students.

However, grading is done by TA, while he is willing and accommodating to help students, the result is that he has little impact on grades.

Awesome class, as long as you put in the effort. Participate in discussions, and read all of the assignments closely and you will get a lot out of this class. I was not his biggest fan in the beginning, but looking back his class was one of the best experiences of freshman year. Nice guy too.

Lots of reading (a reading journal entry or an argument outline sketch due every class) but very detailed, lots of interesting debatesdiscussion, and pretty thought-provoking. Pay attention in class! Work hard and do the readings and it will be rewarding.
Lectures are discussion format, so don't take the class if you're not ready to contribute at least a little. Great coverage of very interesting topics, and not a ton of work overall.
Obvious that he has a passion for teaching and philosophy. Really wants students to learn and understand concepts. But too much work... TAs do pretty much all of the grading.

analysis.name: Neelam Dwivedi
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, but ratings have been improving recently

review_highlights.comment: 350 characters is too short to describe how bad Neelam is. She is the worst student anyone has had by far & looks to punish students. She may be smart in Java but is unable to communicate concepts, is very rude, refuses to explain things looks to fail students and gives 2 quizzes per class via labs and reading quizzes. Run away as fast as you can.

Just had the first test of the semester tonight. You have an hour to code a high level task. It was awful, you can't use anything to help (no googling!). The class is run opposite of how you'd want a programming class to run. You "learn" outside of class and she answers questions inside class about what you learned.

The exams and labs are challenging with time limit but the contents are fair. If you understand the class materials very well, there will be no surprises on the exams or labs. Quizzes are given at the end of the class based on self-assessment and lectures. I highly recommend to take Neelam's class if you want to get a solid foundation in JAVA.

Her homework write-up was so clear that I want all the instructions that I receive in my whole life to be like that. The workload is heavy but since the instructions and TAs are helpful it's a fruitful and satisfying process (if you start working early, otherwise it is hell). Her lecture is also one of the best at Heinz, clear and easy to follow.

If you don't get a correct output of your code, you will almost get 0, no credits for the other parts of the code. TA will not spend time reading your code, if you don't get it right, then you die. Every week has a quiz and a time limit lab. No excuse for absence, if you miss any quiz or lab, you will have a 0, no makeup exam.

HIGHLY recommended.  This class was fun and educational.  Plus, he will be a reference for you after school.

His class is very practical and helpful for me.  I had many wrong understandings of professional writing before and I had been using them in writing all the time before. He corrected many of my wrong concepts although the class is only 6 weeks long.

I came to apprecitate her as a great professor!

Professor Eisenberg is a wonderful professor with a wealth of knowledge to share with her students.  She is certainly a tough grader, but not out of spite.  Prof. Eisenberg is particular, but polite; encouraging, but realistic.  I highly recommend this professor to my peers.
Demands high quality assignments - you can't just write bs down and get credit for it.  Encourages interesting discussions and debates and teaches the material masterfully.

discussion is relaxed, which works well for my style
he knows so much but his classes are so very dull.

totally confusing and ditzy. pretentious and unprofessional.

Dr. Grama was a great professor. Her lectures are very discussion based, so be prepared to read outside of class and obtain required reading material on your own. She was extremely caring and understanding and is very knowledgeable about anthropology.
One of the best professors I've ever taken hands down

review_highlights.comment: Garrett is the kindest person I know. He would help even with material outside of his class and outside of his OH. At times, he would also provide emotional support to get thru the grind.

Garret is great! he's covering the materials at a pace that's easy to digest even for people who have no experience with mathematical proofs before. If you are, you should take 128 instead :P

Highly recommend Ervin for Concepts! He's an amazing lecturer, goes at a pretty good pace, and is a very kind person. It should be relatively easy to get an A in this class as long as you go to office hours for homeworks and do relatively well on assessments. I had an A up until the final which I didn't study for because of various other reasons.

Difficult, but thats because the course material is difficult Ervin makes the material as easy and understandable as possible. I ended up with a B in his class, but could quite easily get an A if I went to class or watched the recordings. Office hours are very helpful, and reading the textbook before lecture is the best preparation, and helpful.

I had Professor Garrett for both 21122 and 21127 . He introduces topics in math gently and takes the time needed to understand the material, instead of just rushing into difficult problems. I'm so glad I took him for concepts, and the subject itself is quite challenging, but he did an excellent job of teaching it. I would gladly have him again.

analysis.name: Erik Thiessen
